text: 2-Chloro-3-hydroxypyridine (2-Cl-pyridin-3-ol), chemical formula C5H4ClNO, molecular weight 129.54, is a single-component organic molecular crystal exhibiting exceptional shear piezoelectric properties. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group Fdd2 (No. 43), with unit cell a = 23.069 Å, b = 25.231 Å, c = 3.8429 Å, Z = 16. The crystal structure is sustained by both hydrogen bonding (O–H···N) and type I halogen–halogen (Cl···Cl) interactions; the non-centrosymmetric polar architecture is compatible with piezoelectricity. Discrete 2-chloro-3-hydroxypyridine molecules are linked by directional hydrogen bonds and halogen–halogen interactions, creating a unique electromechanical anisotropy with high polarization along one crystallographic axis and mechanical flexibility along others. The crystal structure (CCDC 279638, CSD refcode FUFNOJ01) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 298 K with an R factor of 2.95% and a calculated density of 1.539 g/cm³. Density functional theory calculations predicted an exceptionally high shear piezoelectric coefficient d₁₅ = 99.19 pC/N, while experimental piezoresponse force microscopy (PFM) measurements confirmed effective shear piezoelectric responses of 54–74 pC/N and longitudinal coefficients (d₃₃) of 5–10 pC/N, making this compound one of the highest-performing small organic molecular piezoelectrics.
experimental_method: Piezoresponse force microscopy (PFM) - direct and converse piezoelectric measurements
computational_method: Density functional theory (DFT) using VASP with periodic boundary conditions
property_ref_title: Halogen and hydrogen bonded 2-X-pyridin-3-ol (X = Cl, Br, I) organic crystals with large shear piezoelectricity

dft_predictions.methodology: VASP periodic DFT with PBE functional and DFPT for piezoelectric tensors
dft_predictions.basis_set: PAW pseudopotentials with 800 eV plane wave cutoff

experimental_pfm_measurements.measurement_technique: Piezoresponse force microscopy with NT-MDT HA_NC/W2S cantilever
experimental_pfm_measurements.calibration: PFM calibrated against periodically poled lithium niobate (PPLN)

piezoelectric_tensor_comparison.1Cl_vs_ZnO: d15 of 1Cl (99.19 pC/N) ~8.5× higher than ZnO d15 (11.7 pC/N)
piezoelectric_tensor_comparison.1Cl_vs_gamma_glycine: d15 of 1Cl (99.19 pC/N) ~18.6× higher than γ-glycine d15 (5.33 pC/N)
piezoelectric_tensor_comparison.1Cl_vs_PZT: Within 1-2 orders magnitude of commercial PZT (d33 = 350-550 pC/N)
piezoelectric_tensor_comparison.triple_digit_achievement: First small organic molecular crystal approaching triple-digit piezoelectric response

hydrogen_bonding.pattern: Chains running parallel to crystallographic a-axis

halogen_bonding.role: Links anti-parallel hydrogen-bonded chains; sustains structure along b-axis

secondary_interactions.function: Provide structural rigidity along specific crystallographic directions

bonding_and_crystal_structure.crystal_packing: Hydrogen-bonded chains stacked via π-interactions to form layers parallel to ac-plane

mechanical_properties_DFT.anisotropy: High C11 (strong along a-axis due to O–H···N bonds); very low C55 (soft along c-axis due to weak π-interactions)

dielectric_properties.voltage_generation_capability: Up to 2.2 V per unit force and area under shear stress

1I.bonding: Type II I···I interactions (strongest halogen bonding)

isostructurality_and_halogen_series.trend: Halogen substitution modulates piezoelectric response; decreasing d15 with increasing halogen size

design_principles.non_centrosymmetric_requirement: Halogen substitution at position 2 of 3-hydroxypyridine induces non-centrosymmetric crystal packing
design_principles.orthogonal_bonding_axes: O–H···N hydrogen bonds along a-axis; Cl···Cl halogen bonds along b-axis create directional anisotropy
design_principles.flexibility_vs_rigidity_balance: Strong bonding along a and b axes maintains high polarization; weak π-interactions along c-axis enable mechanical compliance and ionic displacement

comparative_performance.vs_ZnO: d33: 1Cl measured 10.63 pC/N vs ZnO 9.9 pC/N (comparable for longitudinal); d15: 1Cl 73.69 vs ZnO not typically quoted for shear
comparative_performance.vs_gamma_glycine: d33: 1Cl 10.63 vs γ-glycine 10.0 pC/N (similar); d15: 1Cl 73.69 vs γ-glycine not typically measured
comparative_performance.vs_organic_perovskites: Comparable to TMCM-MnCl3 d33 (185 pC/N) but much simpler, non-toxic molecule
comparative_performance.application_potential: High voltage constant and brittleness make ideal for vibrational energy harvesting and sensor applications

advantages: Designed from bottom-up crystal engineering principles
High shear piezoelectric response suitable for sensing and actuation

crystal_morphology.BFDH_analysis: Crystal faces (220), (220) show strong O–H···N, Cl···Cl, and C–H interactions—resistant to deformation
crystal_morphology.compliant_faces: Faces (111), (1–11) sustained by weaker π-interactions—prone to deformation under applied force
crystal_morphology.mechanical_implications: Faces along weak bonding directions facilitate ionic displacement, enhancing piezoelectric output

additional_notes: Orthorhombic space group Fdd2 (No. 43) is non-centrosymmetric and polar, satisfying crystallographic requirement for piezoelectricity
FUFNOJ01 crystal structure originally reported by Wardell et al. (2006); piezoelectric properties experimentally validated 2025
R factor (all data) = 3.08%, R factor (gt) = 2.95%, wR factor = 7.64%—high-quality refinement
Z = 16 molecules per unit cell; Z' = 1 molecule in asymmetric unit
Predicted vs measured d15 correlation: R² = 0.9499; slope = 1.1585 (excellent agreement)
DFT-predicted d15 (99.19 pC/N) represents 36% enhancement over experimental measurement (73.69 pC/N), likely due to cantilever buckling in PFM amplifying shear response
Experimental voltage constant g33 = 241 mV·m/N (calculated from PFM d33 and measured dielectric constant)
Dielectric constant measured via impedance analysis: 5.52 (average), with g33 constant of 241 mV·m/N
Halogenation as crystal engineering tool: demonstrates how halogen substitution can induce and modulate piezoelectricity
Isostructural 1Br and 1I allow systematic study of halogen bonding effects on piezoelectric response
Non-halogenated 3-hydroxypyridine (1) shows zero piezoelectric response—confirms halogen atoms essential for non-centrosymmetry
Thermal stability: studied at 298 K; structure robustness across temperature range
Accessible from commercial sources (Sigma-Aldrich for 1Cl and 1Br, ChemScene for 1I); readily crystallized from ethyl acetate solution
Small crystal size and fragility necessitated use of PFM instead of Berlincourt method for measurement
One of the highest shear piezoelectric responses among small organic molecular crystals; approaches performance of inorganic ceramics
Suitable for inclusion in database of experimentally verified high-performance organic piezoelectric molecular crystals
